verses.text: Reuben and Gad ask for the land east of Jordan for their cattle. The request. They like the pasture land.
Reuben and Gad ask for the land east of Jordan for their cattle. The request. They like the pasture land.
Reuben and Gad ask for the land east of Jordan for their cattle. The request. They like the pasture land.
Reuben and Gad ask for the land east of Jordan for their cattle. The request. They like the pasture land.
Reuben and Gad ask for the land east of Jordan for their cattle. The request. They like the pasture land.
Moses fears they will discourage the others, as the spies did. Rebuke. He recalls the old unbelief.
Moses fears they will discourage the others, as the spies did. Rebuke. He recalls the old unbelief.
Moses fears they will discourage the others, as the spies did. Rebuke. He recalls the old unbelief.
Moses fears they will discourage the others, as the spies did. Rebuke. He recalls the old unbelief.
He recalls how God barred that generation, save Caleb and Joshua. Remembered. Unbelief lost the land.
He recalls how God barred that generation, save Caleb and Joshua. Remembered. Unbelief lost the land.
He recalls how God barred that generation, save Caleb and Joshua. Remembered. Unbelief lost the land.
He warns lest they bring God's wrath again. Warning. Discouragement is dangerous.
He warns lest they bring God's wrath again. Warning. Discouragement is dangerous.
He warns lest they bring God's wrath again. Warning. Discouragement is dangerous.
They promise to go armed before Israel until the land is won. The pledge. They will not shirk the war.
They promise to go armed before Israel until the land is won. The pledge. They will not shirk the war.
They promise to go armed before Israel until the land is won. The pledge. They will not shirk the war.
They promise to go armed before Israel until the land is won. The pledge. They will not shirk the war.
Moses agrees, if they keep their word, lest 'your sin will find you out.' Conditional. Duty must be done first.
Moses agrees, if they keep their word, lest 'your sin will find you out.' Conditional. Duty must be done first.
Moses agrees, if they keep their word, lest 'your sin will find you out.' Conditional. Duty must be done first.
Moses agrees, if they keep their word, lest 'your sin will find you out.' Conditional. Duty must be done first.
Moses agrees, if they keep their word, lest 'your sin will find you out.' Conditional. Duty must be done first.
They vow to obey their lord's command. Vowed. They commit to the war.
They vow to obey their lord's command. Vowed. They commit to the war.
They vow to obey their lord's command. Vowed. They commit to the war.
Moses charges Eleazar and Joshua to hold them to it. Charged. The promise is witnessed.
Moses charges Eleazar and Joshua to hold them to it. Charged. The promise is witnessed.
Moses charges Eleazar and Joshua to hold them to it. Charged. The promise is witnessed.
They repeat their pledge to go armed over Jordan. Confirmed. They bind themselves again.
They repeat their pledge to go armed over Jordan. Confirmed. They bind themselves again.
The land is given to Reuben, Gad, and half Manasseh. Given. Their portion is assigned.
The land is given to Reuben, Gad, and half Manasseh. Given. Their portion is assigned.
The land is given to Reuben, Gad, and half Manasseh. Given. Their portion is assigned.
They build cities for their families and folds for their flocks. Built. They settle the land.
They build cities for their families and folds for their flocks. Built. They settle the land.
They build cities for their families and folds for their flocks. Built. They settle the land.
Manasseh's sons take Gilead and its towns. Taken. They possess by conquest.
Manasseh's sons take Gilead and its towns. Taken. They possess by conquest.
